equations.label: eq:exposure
equations.description: China import exposure per worker: weighted average of industry-level import growth by initial employment shares.

equations.label: eq:gravity
equations.description: Gravity equation with importer and exporter fixed effects.

equations.label: eq:labor-demand
equations.description: Local labor demand equation linking employment to wages and import price.

equations.label: eq:bartik
equations.description: Shift-share (Bartik) instrument: local employment shares $z_{ijt}$ interacted with national import growth from other countries.

qa.question: What are the shares in the shift-share instrument?
qa.answer: z_ijt = initial employment share of industry j in CZ i

equations.label: eq:ssiv
equations.description: Shift-share IV estimator in terms of shares $z_{ij}$ and shocks $g_j$.

equations.label: eq:rotemberg
equations.latex: \hat{\beta}_{ss} = \prod_{k=1}^{K}\!\left(\frac{1}{J_k}\right) \sum_j \alpha_j \hat{\beta}_j, \quad \alpha_j = \frac{z_j' M_Z z_j}{\sum_k z_k' M_Z z_k}, \quad M_Z = I - Z(Z'Z)^{-1}Z' = \begin{bmatrix} m_{11} & \cdots & m_{1n} \\ \vdots & \ddots & \vdots \\ m_{n1} & \cdots & m_{nn} \end{bmatrix}
equations.description: Rotemberg weight decomposition: shift-share IV estimator as weighted average of industry-specific IV estimates.

equations.label: eq:reduced-form
equations.description: Reduced form regression of employment change on the instrument (other-country exposure).

equations.label: eq:trade-shock
equations.description: Definition of the China trade shock as the change in US imports from China in industry $j$.

qa.question: What is the main identification strategy?
qa.answer: Shift-share (Bartik) instrument using other high-income countries' import exposure from China

qa.question: What is the main finding for manufacturing employment?
qa.answer: A $1000 increase in China import exposure reduces manufacturing employment by 0.549 percentage points (IV estimate)
